title: baphia capparidifolia subsp. capparidifolia
intro: Baphia capparidifolia subsp. capparidifolia, also known as the forest pea or forest bean, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its pods and seeds, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Baphia capparidifolia subsp. capparidifolia include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
